 {"id":"bd-2iag","title":"Feature: UI Marshaling for Extensions","description":"# Feature: UI Marshaling for Extensions

## Status

**Implementation tracked in: bd-1u4t (Task: Implement UI Hostcall Handler)**

This feature's detailed implementation (UiContext trait, TuiUiContext, hostcall dispatch) has been consolidated into the existing task bd-1u4t which already had the correct dependency chain.

## Original Scope

Extensions can trigger UI updates via pi.ui:
- Spinners (show/update/stop)
- Progress bars (show/increment/finish)
- Notifications (info/warn/error)
- Status line
- User prompts (askUser, select, confirm, input)

 {"id":"bd-2ikc","title":"Editor: @ fuzzy file reference insertion","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `@` file reference UX: typing `@` triggers fuzzy-search project files.

# Behavioral Spec
- As the user types after `@`, suggestions narrow using fuzzy matching.
- Selecting a file inserts a usable reference into the editor.

# Open Design Choice (resolve in implementation)
Legacy mentions “File reference: type `@` to fuzzy-search project files”.
We must choose one of:
1) Insert `@relative/path` (explicit marker), OR
2) Insert `relative/path` but still treat it as an attachment later.

Pick one and document it; ensure it works with existing file-context inclusion code.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can attach files without leaving the keyboard.
- [ ] Inserted reference is correct relative to cwd and works across platforms.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-2jdz","title":"Interactive: implement /new (start new session without restart)","description":"# Goal
Implement `/new` inside interactive mode to start a fresh session without restarting the process.

# Required Behavior
- Clear the current conversation view.
- Create a new Session:
  - new session id
  - new JSONL file path (if persistence enabled)
  - header uses current cwd, model/thinking settings
- Reset agent message history to an empty session context.

# UX
- Preserve loaded resources (skills/prompts/themes) from current run.
- Provide a status message indicating new session started.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/new` results in a clean slate session.
- [ ] Persistence works (new file created, not overwriting old).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

-{"id":"bd-2jh","title":"Implement error handling path tests","description":"# Implement error handling path tests

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures
- API rate limits
- Authentication failures
- Malformed responses
- File system errors
- Timeout scenarios

## Runtime + Determinism
- Do not introduce new tokio-only tests; use `#[asupersync::test]` or a runtime wrapper.
- All API error cases must use VCR playback (no live network).
- File system errors must use temp dirs and deterministic paths.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml).
- Log cassette path, error category, and sanitized error details.
- On failure, dump expected vs actual error variants + any response body fragments.

## Test Categories

### Network Errors
```rust
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_connection_refused() {
    let vcr = VcrRecorder::new("network_connection_refused");
    let provider = AnthropicProvider::new_with_client(vcr.client());

    let result = provider.stream(&context, &options).await;
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(Error::Network { .. })));
}
```

### API Errors
- 429 rate limit (retry-after)
- 401 auth failure
- 403 forbidden
- 400 bad request
- 500 server error
- 529 overloaded

### Malformed Response Errors
- Invalid JSON body
- Missing required fields
- Unexpected SSE event type
- Truncated stream

### File System Errors
- Read permission denied
- Write permission denied
- Disk full (simulate with tempfs limits if feasible)
- Path too long

### Tool Execution Errors
- bash command not found
- bash timeout
- edit file not found

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure for API error testing)

## Files
- tests/error_handling.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 25+ error handling tests
- [ ] All network errors covered (via VCR/local simulation)
- [ ] All API error codes covered
- [ ] File system errors covered
- [ ] Tool errors covered
- [ ] Logs include error category + cassette id
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] All tests pass","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:33.110276433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:02:46.593517276Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-30u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2jh","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Goal
Test all error conditions that can occur during normal operation, with detailed logging.

## Background
Error paths are currently untested but critical for UX:
- Network failures

 {"id":"bd-2ke","title":"Impl: QuickJS promise bridge + job draining semantics","description":"# Goal
Wire QuickJS async runtime to the PiJS event loop with a **correct Promise/hostcall bridge** and explicit job draining.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `host.call` returns a JS Promise backed by a Rust completion handle.
- Job draining loop: run pending jobs, then poll timers/hostcalls per spec.
- Cancellation propagation: cancel token to hostcall, reject Promise with mapped error.
- Explicit error mapping + stack traces for debugging.
- Structured logs per hostcall with correlation IDs.

# Correctness Notes
- No re-entrancy hazards: only drain jobs at safe points.
- Promise resolution order stable with scheduler sequence ids.

# Tests
- Unit tests for Promise resolution ordering.
- Conformance fixtures for cancellation/timeouts.
- E2E lifecycle harness asserts log ordering.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:13.010362542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:47.069742360Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:01:18.379160588Z","close_reason":"Implemented QuickJS promise bridge with job draining semantics. Created PiJsRuntime combining QuickJS + Scheduler + Promise bridge.  {"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

Implementation beads:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0
- node:child_process subset: bd-2sr
- WebAssembly bridge (PiWasm): bd-1ry

# Tests
- Unit transform fixtures for common imports and injection.
- Negative tests for forbidden APIs with exact messages.
- E2E harness verifies rewritten bundles run 16/16 with actionable failures.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics (bd-123) and hostcall ABI (bd-37z).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:59.736950487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:25.341887112Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124743731Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added §2A Extc Compatibility Contract to EXTENSIONS.md with canonical rewrite rules, global polyfill injection, forbidden/flagged APIs, compatibility matrix, and sourcemap contract","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kj0","title":"Error UX: render hints in interactive/print/RPC modes","description":"# Goal
Surface the hint mapping to users consistently across all app modes.

# Scope
- Interactive TUI:
  - when rendering an error panel/message, include (optionally) the hint lines.
- Print mode:
  - stderr formatting includes hint lines.
- RPC mode:
  - include hints as a structured field (if protocol supports) or in a dedicated error message section.

# UX Requirements
- Hints should be visually separated from the main error message.
- Default behavior: show hints when available.
- Keep output stable for tests.

# Dependencies
- Depends on the hint mapping definition task.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one representative error per subsystem shows a hint.
- [ ] Output remains readable (no wall-of-text).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
